articles.title: Tesla Autonomy Making Streets Safer in Europe
articles.description: ROME/PARIS/BERLIN – Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) Supervised system, the product of Elon Musk’s relentless 12-year focus on end-to-end neural networks, is now being experienced by European leaders in a series of high-profile demonstrations that have no rivals. Rome Mayor First to Ride Elon’s Vision in Italy On Wednesday, Rome Mayor Roberto Gualtieri rode as a […]
articles.author: Gail Alfar

articles.description:
Rivian is giving owners of its second-generation R1T and R1S an easier, more seamless way to unlock and drive their vehicles. Later this month, the automaker will roll out its .46 over-the-air (OTA) software update, […]

articles.title: SpaceX Launches Starlink Service in South Korea
articles.description:
SpaceX’s Starlink has officially launched its high-speed satellite internet service across South Korea today. The launch follows regulatory approvals granted earlier this year that allowed foreign satellite operators to provide broadband services in South Korea. […]

The post SpaceX Launches Starlink Service in South Korea appeared first on Drive Tesla.

articles.title: SpaceX Preps ‘Starlink Mobile’ Launch with New Trademark
articles.description:
SpaceX appears to be gearing up for a major push into the mobile carrier market. According to longtime Tesla and SpaceX watcher @SERobinsonJr, the company has officially filed a U.S. trademark for “Starlink Mobile,” covering a wide range of telecommunications services — including video and data to mobile phones, smart devices, and full-fledged cellular communication […]

The post SpaceX Preps ‘Starlink Mobile’ Launch with New Trademark appeared first on TeslaNorth.com.

articles.title: Tesla FSD 14.2.1 Lets You Text and Drive — Sometimes
articles.description:
Tesla’s latest Full Self-Driving (Supervised) update, version 14.2.1, is already raising eyebrows — and not for its driving behaviour, but for what it seems to allow drivers to do. According to Tesla owner and watcher @Teslaconomics, the new update appears to let drivers text while the system handles the wheel. “Wait… am I able to […]

The post Tesla FSD 14.2.1 Lets You Text and Drive — Sometimes appeared first on TeslaNorth.com.

articles.title: Tesla Cracks Consumer Reports’ Top 10 for First Time
articles.description:
Tesla has cracked Consumer Reports’ annual top 10 auto brands list for the first time ever, marking a major shift for the EV maker as it continues refining both quality and affordability. The 2026 Automotive Brand Report Card, published by Consumer Reports magazine and detailed by the Detroit Free Press, places Tesla at No. 10 […]

articles.title: Tesla Pulls Autopilot from Inventory Cars, Adds FSD Trial
articles.description:
Tesla appears to be quietly phasing out Autopilot on certain pre-owned 2026 Model Y inventory vehicles in the U.S., raising fresh questions about the company’s long-term plans for its driver-assistance stack. The change was first spotted by Tesla enthusiast and Cybertruck owner @Feelsogoodme, who noticed multiple 2026 Model Y listings with no reference to Autopilot […]

The post Tesla Pulls Autopilot from Inventory Cars, Adds FSD Trial appeared first on TeslaNorth.com.
articles.author: Nehal Malik

articles.title: Tesla Model Y L in China Now Sold Out Until February 2026
articles.description:
Tesla’s new six-seat Model Y L continues to post massive demand in China, with the extended-wheelbase variant now fully sold out through January 2026. Estimated delivery dates for new orders have officially slipped into February 2026, according to updated guidance on Tesla’s online configurator and data shared by well-known industry tracker @Tslachan on X. This […]

The post Tesla Model Y L in China Now Sold Out Until February 2026 appeared first on TeslaNorth.com.
